highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142 Released
highlights.summary: The latest Claude Code release adds several new flags to the `claude agents` command, letting you configure background sessions with options like `--model`, `--effort`, `--permission-mode`, and more. Fast mode also now defaults to the newer Opus 4.7 model instead of Opus 4.6, so your sessions will automatically benefit from the latest model improvements.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View and /goal Command
highlights.summary: This release introduces two powerful features: the Agent View (`claude agents`) gives you a single dashboard to track all running, blocked, and completed Claude Code sessions at a glance. The new `/goal` command lets you define a completion condition and Claude will continue working autonomously across multiple steps until that goal is met.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the model now used by default in Claude Code's Fast mode. Knowing the underlying model has changed helps you understand capability improvements you may notice in code generation, reasoning, and multi-step task completion.

highlights.title: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post covers best practices for using Claude Code effectively when your project grows beyond a few files, including where to start when onboarding Claude to an existing codebase. It's a practical read for anyone who has hit the limits of simple prompting and wants Claude to navigate complex project structures more reliably.

highlights.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
highlights.summary: This viral post explores how Claude Code excels at generating and iterating on plain HTML — often producing surprisingly complete, usable results faster than heavier frameworks. It's a great reminder that starting simple with HTML prototypes can be a highly effective way to validate ideas quickly with AI assistance.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a fast-paced walkthrough of Claude Code from basic setup to more advanced usage patterns, all in under 20 minutes. It's an ideal starting point if you want a concise video introduction without committing to a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: Show HN: I Built a 500K LOC Production App Alone in 7 Months
highlights.summary: A developer shares a 5-hour uncut live session demonstrating how they built a 500,000-line production app solo using AI coding tools including Claude Code. The discussion thread is valuable for understanding real-world AI-assisted development workflows, limitations, and the discipline required to manage a large codebase with AI help.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.141: Hook Terminal Sequences and HTTPS Plugin Cloning
highlights.summary: This release adds a `terminalSequence` field to hook JSON output, enabling hooks to send desktop notifications, update window titles, or trigger terminal bells — even without an active terminal. It also introduces `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_PREFER_HTTPS` for cloning GitHub plugins over HTTPS instead of SSH, which is helpful in CI/CD or restricted network environments.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/goal` command to define a clear, measurable completion condition before starting a complex task — Claude will keep iterating autonomously until your goal is met, saving you from manually re-prompting at each step.

When working in large codebases, give Claude Code a brief 'map' of your project structure in your first message (e.g., key folders, entry points, naming conventions). This context helps Claude navigate and make fewer wrong assumptions right from the start.

If you're prototyping a new feature or UI idea, try asking Claude Code to generate a plain HTML file first. You'll get a working visual prototype in seconds that you can iterate on, before committing to a full framework implementation.
